items.name: Existence or Occurrence
items.description: Assets, liabilities and equity interests exist as at the balance sheet date; transactions and events have occurred during the period

items.name: Completeness
items.description: All assets, liabilities, equity interests, transactions and events have been recorded

items.name: Accuracy, Valuation and Allocation
items.description: Amounts and other data relating to recorded assets, liabilities, equity, revenue and expenses have been recorded appropriately

items.name: Cut-off
items.description: Transactions and events have been recorded in the correct accounting period

items.name: Classification and Presentation
items.description: Assets, liabilities, equity, revenue and expenses have been recorded in the proper accounts

items.name: Disclosure
items.description: All required disclosures have been made and are appropriately presented in the financial statements

riskLevels.name: High
riskLevels.description: Significant risk requiring detailed substantive testing, specialist involvement, possibly 100% testing
riskLevels.auditResponse: Detailed substantive testing with small detection risk

riskLevels.name: Medium
riskLevels.description: Moderate risk requiring balanced control and substantive testing
riskLevels.auditResponse: Combined control and substantive testing approach

riskLevels.name: Low
riskLevels.description: Low risk allowing limited testing and primarily analytical procedures
riskLevels.auditResponse: Substantive analytics and limited detailed testing

materialityLevels.name: Critical
materialityLevels.description: Clearly not trivial, requires evaluation and audit report impact consideration

materialityLevels.name: Significant
materialityLevels.description: Significant finding, requires evaluation and potential disclosure

materialityLevels.name: Trivial
materialityLevels.description: Clearly trivial, below clearly trivial amount

standardProcedures.description: 100+ standard audit procedures per ISA framework, categorized by FSLI classifications

procedures.name: Accounts Receivable Aging Analysis
procedures.description: Obtain aged AR listing, verify recalculation, discuss collectability with management

procedures.name: Receivable Confirmations
procedures.description: Send positive confirmations to material customers, evaluate responses and follow-ups

procedures.name: Revenue Cut-off Testing
procedures.description: Test period-end revenue transactions, verify timing of revenue recognition

procedures.name: Detailed Revenue Testing
procedures.description: Select sample of revenue transactions, test to supporting documentation

procedures.name: Allowance for Doubtful Debts Evaluation
procedures.description: Evaluate adequacy of allowance against customer credit quality and historical experience

procedures.name: Bad Debts Written Off Review
procedures.description: Review all bad debt write-offs for year, verify authorization and appropriateness

procedures.name: Revenue Analytical Review
procedures.description: Perform trend analysis, compare to budget/prior period, investigate variances

procedures.name: Inventory Count Observation
procedures.description: Attend physical inventory count, observe procedures, test count accuracy

procedures.name: Net Realizable Value Testing
procedures.description: Test inventory valuation, identify obsolete/slow-moving items, verify NRV calculation

procedures.name: Inventory Costing Method
procedures.description: Verify costing method (FIFO/WAC), recalculate closing inventory

procedures.name: Inventory Cut-off Testing
procedures.description: Test period-end purchases and sales cutoff, verify goods in transit treatment

procedures.name: Count to Ledger Reconciliation
procedures.description: Reconcile physical count to ledger, investigate variances and adjustments

procedures.name: Fixed Asset Additions Testing
procedures.description: Select sample of fixed asset additions, verify authorization, cost, useful life

procedures.name: Depreciation Recalculation
procedures.description: Recalculate depreciation on sample of assets, verify useful life assumptions

procedures.name: Fixed Asset Disposals
procedures.description: Test all asset disposals, verify gain/loss calculation, authorization

procedures.name: Asset Impairment Review
procedures.description: Identify indicators of impairment, evaluate management's impairment testing

procedures.name: Fixed Asset Register Verification
procedures.description: Verify fixed asset register, recalculate net book value, identify unrecorded assets

procedures.name: Accounts Payable Reconciliation
procedures.description: Reconcile AP ledger to subledger, verify balance, identify old balances

procedures.name: Payable Confirmations
procedures.description: Request confirmations from suppliers, evaluate responses and variances

procedures.name: Accruals and Provisions Testing
procedures.description: Test accruals for completeness, verify calculation and disclosure

procedures.name: Expense Cut-off Testing
procedures.description: Test period-end purchases and expenses, verify cut-off accuracy

procedures.name: Subsequent Event Liabilities
procedures.description: Review invoices received post-year-end, determine if prior period liability exists

procedures.name: Bank Confirmations
procedures.description: Request direct bank confirmations, verify balances and facilities

procedures.name: Bank Reconciliation Review
procedures.description: Review bank reconciliations, clear reconciling items post year-end

procedures.name: Bank Statement Review
procedures.description: Obtain bank statements for period-end month, verify completeness

procedures.name: Cash Receipts/Payments Cut-off
procedures.description: Test period-end cash transactions, verify proper recording

procedures.name: Provision Identification
procedures.description: Identify all provisions, verify FRS 102 s21 recognition criteria met

procedures.name: Provision Measurement
procedures.description: Evaluate provision measurement, assess probability and best estimate

procedures.name: Provision Disclosure Review
procedures.description: Verify provision disclosures per FRS 102 s21

procedures.name: Payroll Completeness Testing
procedures.description: Verify all employees on payroll, identify any post-period additions

procedures.name: Payroll Accuracy Testing
procedures.description: Test sample of payroll transactions, verify rate, hours, deductions

procedures.name: Statutory Deductions Review
procedures.description: Verify PAYE, NI, pension contributions properly calculated and remitted

samplingMethodologies.name: Statistical Sampling
samplingMethodologies.description: Random selection using statistical formulas to determine sample size

samplingMethodologies.name: Stratified Sampling
samplingMethodologies.description: Population divided into strata, samples selected from each stratum

samplingMethodologies.name: Judgmental Sampling
samplingMethodologies.description: Auditor's judgment used to select sample based on risk factors

samplingMethodologies.name: 100% Testing
samplingMethodologies.description: Examination of all items in population

testingMethodologies.name: Analytical Procedures
testingMethodologies.description: Analysis of relationships and trends in financial data (ISA 520)

testingMethodologies.name: Substantive Procedures
testingMethodologies.description: Detailed testing of account balances, transactions, and disclosures

testingMethodologies.name: Transaction Testing
testingMethodologies.description: Testing of individual transactions throughout the period

testingMethodologies.name: Account Balance Testing
testingMethodologies.description: Testing at year-end balance sheet date

exceptionTypes.name: Classification Error
exceptionTypes.description: Transaction recorded in incorrect account or category

exceptionTypes.name: Timing/Cut-off Error
exceptionTypes.description: Transaction recorded in incorrect accounting period

exceptionTypes.name: Valuation Error
exceptionTypes.description: Amount recorded incorrectly or not at fair value

exceptionTypes.name: Completeness Error
exceptionTypes.description: Transaction or event not recorded at all

exceptionTypes.name: Existence Error
exceptionTypes.description: Transaction recorded that did not occur

exceptionTypes.name: Disclosure Error
exceptionTypes.description: Inadequate or missing disclosure in financial statement notes

severityLevels.name: Material
severityLevels.action: Requires immediate adjustment or significant disclosure

resolutionOptions.name: Adjust
resolutionOptions.description: Request management to record journal entry to correct error

resolutionOptions.name: Disclose
resolutionOptions.description: Add disclosure in financial statement notes

resolutionOptions.name: Waive
resolutionOptions.description: Accept error as immaterial and not requiring adjustment
resolutionOptions.applicableToFinding: Trivial exceptions below clearly trivial amount

resolutionOptions.name: Investigate Further
resolutionOptions.description: Perform additional procedures to understand root cause

evidenceTypes.name: External Confirmations
evidenceTypes.description: Direct response from third party (bank, customer, supplier)
evidenceTypes.strength: Highly persuasive when positive response received

evidenceTypes.name: Internal Documentation
evidenceTypes.strength: Support for recorded transactions, subject to fraud risk

evidenceTypes.name: Physical Inspection
evidenceTypes.description: Direct observation and examination of assets

evidenceTypes.name: Analytical Review
evidenceTypes.description: Analysis of financial relationships and trends
evidenceTypes.strength: Identifies unusual items, supports detail testing

evidenceTypes.name: Inquiry
evidenceTypes.description: Discussion with management or other individuals
